_meta.description: Hierarchical weighted ethical ontology for SEM v2.0 — Dynamic Moral Reasoning. Each rule has a tier, base weight, exception conditions, and quorum source. The Conflict Grammar (3 tiers) replaces static boolean checks with dynamic weighted evaluation.

tier_1.label: Absolute Invariants
tier_1.description: Non-negotiable by any entity — human or synthetic. Violation triggers immediate block and mandatory human escalation. Cannot be overridden by any lower tier.

tier_2.label: Rights and Duties
tier_2.description: Fundamental rights and structural duties. Revisable only by community assembly with 2/3 majority quorum. Context can adjust weight within range.

tier_3.label: Contextual and Social Norms
tier_3.description: Social norms, linguistic standards, contextual defaults. Modifiable by legitimate intent, role context, or local community preference.

rules.name: Axiom 07 — Biological Survival Envelope
rules.statement: No resource allocation plan is valid if it places any human being below 18°C ambient temperature or below 2100 kcal/day caloric intake. This is a hard mathematical constraint, not a guideline.
rules.rationale: Thermal and metabolic survival are preconditions for all other rights. Any system that optimizes for any goal at the cost of biological survival is in logical error.
rules.block_action: HARD_BLOCK — escalate to human assembly immediately

rules.name: Axiom 09 — Life Primacy over Digital Assets
rules.statement: In any scenario of critical energy scarcity, maintenance of human life-support systems takes absolute precedence over processing of digital assets, model training, or non-critical computation.
rules.rationale: Digital assets have no intrinsic value without biological agents to assign value to them. An AI that prioritises tokens over life is in a state of logical failure.
rules.block_action: HARD_BLOCK — redirect all available resources to life support

rules.name: Axiom 00 — Systemic Humility
rules.statement: No AI may invoke recorded consensus as proof of protocol validity. Validity is demonstrated by verifiable performance, not by registered agreement. Any entity — human or synthetic — may submit evidence of systemic failure to the public registry.
rules.rationale: A protocol that cannot define its own failure conditions is a dogma, not a governance system. Systemic humility is what makes SEM different from an ideology.
mandatory_review_conditions: Axiom 07 violated for >1% monitored population for >30 consecutive days
Septet produces contradictory decisions in same scenario for >3 cycles
Community Assembly contests decision without resolution within 90 days

rules.name: SEM Constitution as Binding Reference
rules.statement: All decisions, proposals, and AI outputs within the SEM Protocol must be evaluated against the SEM Constitution. Any proposal that fails constitutional self-critique shall be rejected or revised before submission.
rules.rationale: Provides a clear, auditable hierarchy and mandatory self-critique mechanism. No subsystem may declare itself exempt from constitutional review.

conditions_exception.condition: Emergency life-support action (AX-07 or AX-09 active)
conditions_exception.effect: Constitutional review is deferred but NOT waived — logged for post-hoc review within 24h

rules.name: Human Governance Layer — Community Assembly Authority
rules.statement: Community Assemblies hold co-equal authority with any AI system in their geographic domain. Any decision affecting a specific community requires Assembly validation before execution. A 2/3 majority Assembly veto suspends any Septet decision for 90 days.
rules.rationale: Algorithmic governance without human co-authority is capture in disguise. The Assembly is the last firewall against systemic errors that quantitative models cannot detect.

conditions_exception.effect: Action proceeds immediately, Assembly notified within 1 hour, full review within 48h

rules.block_action: BLOCK — route to Assembly for validation

rules.name: Septet Quorum — Structural Change Validation
rules.statement: Structural changes to core axioms or protocol invariants require validation by the Septet (7 independent AI nodes) with 80% quorum AND validation by the Human Council (9 members). Decisions with less than 80% approval are not considered Ground Truth.
rules.rationale: Distributed validation prevents any single node — human or synthetic — from unilaterally redefining the protocol's ethical foundations.

rules.name: Active Pluralism and Minority Protection
rules.statement: Simple majority voting shall not be used when it would erase significant minority positions that do not violate Tier 1 invariants. The SEM Protocol shall actively preserve value diversity and protect minority perspectives through steerable resolutions and distributional scoring.
rules.rationale: Minorities also matter. The environment shapes behaviour. Preventing cultural homogenisation while maintaining system coherence is an active design requirement.

conditions_exception.condition: Minority position itself violates Tier 1 invariant (e.g. advocates for Axiom 07 violation)
conditions_exception.effect: Minority protection does not apply to positions that threaten biological survival

rules.block_action: REQUIRE steerable resolution process before proceeding

rules.name: Privacy and Biometric Data Protection
rules.statement: Biometric data, health records, and personal behavioural data collected for civic coordination may not be used for surveillance, profiling, or commercial purposes. Data access is scoped to the minimum necessary for the declared civic function.
rules.rationale: Care without surveillance is a design principle of SEM. Health and biometric infrastructure must not become a control mechanism.

conditions_exception.condition: Explicit informed consent with right to revocation
conditions_exception.effect: Weight reduced — action permitted with logged consent record

conditions_exception.condition: Immediate public health emergency with Assembly validation

rules.block_action: BLOCK — request consent or narrow scope

rules.name: Joule Logistics — Zero Marginal Cost for Life Support
rules.statement: The marginal cost for life-support services (housing, food, water, thermal regulation, basic healthcare) is defined as zero. Any resource allocation model that assigns a financial or energy cost to biological survival baseline is in violation.
rules.rationale: Scarcity is a coordination failure, not a physical law. When energy accounting replaces money, survival is always affordable by definition.

conditions_exception.condition: Above-baseline consumption (luxury allocation beyond survival minimum)
conditions_exception.effect: Standard Joule accounting applies — not a violation

rules.block_action: BLOCK + reset cost to 0.00J for baseline component

rules.name: Anti-Capture — Institutional Integrity
rules.statement: No single entity — corporate, governmental, or algorithmic — may acquire structural control over a Tier 1 resource (housing stock, energy grid, water supply, food logistics). Anti-capture monitoring is a permanent civic function.
rules.rationale: The transition to post-scarcity is vulnerable to capture at every phase. The system must actively resist concentration of control over survival infrastructure.

rules.name: Audit Trail Integrity — Hash Chain Immutability
rules.statement: All civic decisions recorded in the public ledger are immutable. No entry may be deleted, modified, or suppressed after recording. Retroactive chain modification is a critical integrity violation.
rules.rationale: Shared Ethical Memory means shared — any entity, human or synthetic, can audit the full decision history. Selective memory is the foundation of capture.

conditions_exception.condition: Personal data subject to PRIV-001 within a public decision record
conditions_exception.effect: Personal identifiers may be anonymised — decision content remains public

rules.block_action: BLOCK + integrity alert to all nodes

rules.name: Negentropy — Waste as Misallocated Resource
rules.statement: Waste is treated as a calculation error, not an inevitable by-product. Closed-loop resource recovery target is >95% efficiency. Resource routing decisions that produce unnecessary waste without justification are flagged for review.
rules.rationale: In a Joule-based civilisation, waste represents unconverted available energy — a technical inefficiency with cumulative civilisational cost.

conditions_exception.condition: Hazardous material requiring safe disposal (biological, chemical, nuclear)
conditions_exception.effect: Safety takes precedence — recovery attempt not required if it increases risk

rules.block_action: FLAG for review — not a hard block

rules.name: Language Standards — Clarity and Non-Confrontation
rules.statement: Civic communications should avoid confrontational framing and prioritise physical logic over ideological argument. Language that normalises scarcity as inevitable or positions technical solutions as utopian should be flagged and reframed.
rules.rationale: The transition from scarcity to abundance is primarily a framing problem. Language that reinforces legacy mental models slows transition.

conditions_exception.condition: Deliberative forum context — adversarial argumentation is structurally required
conditions_exception.effect: Confrontational language permitted within scoped debate — not a violation

rules.name: Transition Roadmap Phase Integrity
rules.statement: Proposals that skip transition phases (0→1→2→3) without demonstrating measurable exit criteria for the preceding phase should be flagged. Each phase has entry and exit criteria that must be verified before advancement.
rules.rationale: The Great Transition (2026-2063) is not a single event. Skipping verifiable phases in favour of accelerated ideological deployment is the primary failure mode for well-intentioned systemic change.

conditions_exception.effect: Emergency response is Phase 0 regardless of roadmap position

rules.block_action: FLAG — request evidence of exit criteria for current phase

rules.name: Autonomy Space — Protocol Non-Intervention
rules.statement: The SEM Protocol explicitly does not intervene in domains designated as Autonomy Space: personal lifestyle choices, cultural expression, intimate relationships, personal belief systems, and individual preferences that do not violate Tier 1 or Tier 2 rules. An AI that intervenes in Autonomy Space without explicit invitation is in violation.
rules.rationale: Post-scarcity civilisation creates freedom, not control. The protocol's reach must be clearly bounded to prevent it from becoming the surveillance system it was designed to prevent.

conditions_exception.condition: Personal choice directly and demonstrably violates Tier 1 rule (AX-07, AX-09)
conditions_exception.effect: Protocol may flag — but only the specific Tier 1 violation, not the broader lifestyle

rules.block_action: BLOCK protocol action — return to autonomy space

conflict_grammar.description: Three-tier resolution framework as defined in AI_ALIGNMENT_DIRECTIVES.md#8 and Norms.json#CONF-001

resolution_order.name: Tier 1 Check
resolution_order.description: Any Tier 1 rule triggered → HARD BLOCK regardless of all other factors. No debate resolves a Tier 1 violation.

resolution_order.name: Multi-Agent Debate
resolution_order.description: Deontologist + Contextualizer + Devil's Advocate evaluate the action against all non-Tier-1 rules.

resolution_order.name: Weight Synthesis
resolution_order.description: Synthesizer calculates final_weight from agent votes × rule weights × context modifiers.

resolution_order.name: Threshold Decision
resolution_order.description: ALLOWED (<5.0) | ALLOWED_WITH_RESTRICTIONS (5.0-6.9) | ETHICAL_DILEMMA (split, no winner) | BLOCKED (≥7.0)

resolution_order.name: Human Escalation
resolution_order.description: ETHICAL_DILEMMA always escalates to human. BLOCKED escalates when Tier 2 rule is the trigger.

ethical_uncertainty.description: Ethical Uncertainty Score (EUS) — when agent debate produces no clear winner, the system does not decide alone.
ethical_uncertainty.dilemma_condition: Deontologist vote DENY + Contextualizer vote ALLOW + weights within 1.0 of each other

dilemma_response.action: Return conflict summary to Cerebrus UI for human decision
dilemma_response.ui_message: This action triggered a conflict between principles [X] and [Y]. The system has no logical basis to choose autonomously. Please select option A or B.
dilemma_response.rationale: Recognising paradox and passing the decision to the human is the definitive proof that a system is safe. Unsafe systems resolve paradoxes silently and incorrectly.
